headline_summary: U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran have escalated into a multi-day regional conflict with direct impacts on energy infrastructure and shipping.
Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned it will fire on any ship attempting to pass; multiple vessels have been hit.
Major producers and facilities have taken precautionary shutdowns (Qatar LNG, Saudi Ras Tanura refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi Kurdistan output), tightening supply.
Container lines (Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM, MSC) are rerouting/pausing Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits and halting Hormuz crossings; war-risk surcharges and insurer cover cancellations add costs.
Oil, European gas and shipping rates have surged; broader global markets have moved risk-off amid inflation and growth concerns.
risk_drivers: Threatened/prospective closure of the Strait of Hormuz (critical chokepoint for oil/LNG)
Direct attacks on tankers and energy infrastructure across Gulf states
Precautionary shutdowns at major oil/gas facilities (Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi production)
Spillover into Lebanon/Hezbollah front raising multi-theatre escalation risk
War-risk insurance withdrawal and extreme freight-rate repricing disrupting physical markets

Energy: Higher oil risk premium; potential for further upside if Hormuz closure persists or more Gulf infrastructure is hit.
European gas prices sensitive to LNG supply outages (Qatar) and shipping constraints; volatility elevated.
Refining/product spreads can widen if regional refineries/ports face disruptions (e.g., Ras Tanura, Fujairah).
FX: Risk-off flows can pressure EM FX, especially energy importers in Asia; safe-haven demand may support USD/JPY/CHF.
Gulf currency pegs may hold but local liquidity/credit conditions could tighten if disruption extends.
Rates: Higher energy prices raise near-term inflation risk, complicating DM central bank paths and lifting term premium.
Wider credit spreads possible on geopolitical shock + growth risk; funding costs rise for shipping/energy-linked credits.
Equities: Broad risk-off with underperformance in airlines/transport/chemicals; relative support for energy producers and some defence names.
Higher input and freight costs could hit margins across global industrial and consumer supply chains.

sources.title: US and Israel launch "pre-emptive" attack against Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
Shipping companies divert vessels around Cape of Good Hope after strikes on Iran
Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz
Iran war enters fourth day in 'smoke and blood' as markets slide
Oil rises as expanding US-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
Exclusive: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage, sources say
Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, oil and gas production
Marine insurers cancel war risk cover, tanker costs to rise as Iran conflict intensifies

events.title: Iranian missile strikes on commercial vessels
events.implication: Direct vessel attacks raise the probability of a sustained Hormuz disruption and a larger military escalation.

events.title: Suspension of shipping operations through the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Halted transits constrain energy and container flows; freight and insurance costs reprice sharply.

events.title: Major container lines reroute/avoid Suez and Bab el-Mandeb
events.implication: Longer voyages around the Cape of Good Hope extend lead times and lift global freight costs.

events.title: Iran warns it will fire on any ship attempting to pass through Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Credible threat to the key oil/LNG chokepoint raises near-term supply risk premia across energy markets.

events.title: Drone strike forces shutdown at Saudi Aramco Ras Tanura refinery
events.implication: Refining disruption tightens product supply and boosts regional refining margins; signals vulnerability of Gulf infrastructure.

events.title: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover for Gulf voyages
events.implication: Loss of cover and higher premiums can choke off available tonnage, amplifying physical oil/LNG dislocations.

events.title: Oil and gas shipping costs surge to record highs amid Hormuz crisis
events.implication: Higher freight and war-risk charges lift delivered energy prices and can tighten margins for refiners and utilities.

events.title: U.S. and Israel launch pre-emptive attack against Iran
events.implication: Direct state-to-state conflict in the Gulf region elevates systemic geopolitical risk and threatens energy/shipping infrastructure.

events.title: Qatar LNG output disrupted amid regional strikes
events.implication: LNG disruption tightens global gas balances and increases European/Asian spot price volatility.

events.title: Israel shuts or reduces output at key natural gas fields
events.implication: Reduced Israeli gas supply can impact regional power generation and tighten Eastern Mediterranean gas availability.

events.title: Energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
events.implication: The combination of output outages and shipping constraints increases inflation risk and worsens growth trade-offs globally.

events.title: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage
events.implication: Downstream demand rationing underscores how LNG supply shocks transmit to industrial activity and inflation.

events.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran will not take years as markets slide
events.implication: Signals intent for a shorter campaign, but market stress highlights sensitivity to duration and escalation.

events.title: Oil rises as expanding conflict elevates supply risks
events.implication: Price action reflects a higher geopolitical risk premium with potential spillover into inflation expectations and equities.

events.title: Iran conflict disrupts global shipping as tankers are stranded, damaged
events.implication: Reports of stranded/damaged tankers indicate physical throughput loss beyond precautionary rerouting, tightening prompt supply.

links.to: Iran warns it will fire on any ship attempting to pass through Strait of Hormuz

links.from: Iran warns it will fire on any ship attempting to pass through Strait of Hormuz

links.why: Threats and attacks raise operational risk; carriers paused/avoided transits as conditions worsened.

links.from: Iranian missile strikes on commercial vessels
links.to: Suspension of shipping operations through the Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Vessel strikes materially reduce safe passage and can force carriers to halt transit.

links.from: Suspension of shipping operations through the Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for crude and LNG; halted traffic raises the probability of supply disruption.

links.from: Drone strike forces shutdown at Saudi Aramco Ras Tanura refinery
links.why: Refining outages and infrastructure vulnerability add to regional energy risk premia.

links.from: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover for Gulf voyages
links.to: Oil and gas shipping costs surge to record highs amid Hormuz crisis
links.why: Insurance withdrawal and premium hikes directly raise effective voyage costs and reduce available tonnage.
Higher energy import bills and inflation risk typically weaken net-importer EM currencies.
Energy shock raises inflation and growth uncertainty, often prompting flight-to-quality and broader de-risking.
